story_assessments.story_title: Thinking Machines makes open weights newly visible again
story_assessments.target_audience: Creators, small teams, AI builders and learners who want more control over models
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want a model they can customize, fine-tune and deploy with less vendor lock-in.
story_assessments.interest_signal: A new open-weights multimodal model is available now and can be tried on Tinker today.
story_assessments.stop_reason: It is a concrete model release you can test instead of another abstract AI announcement.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A tunable multimodal model may fit specific workflows better than a closed system you cannot modify.
story_assessments.proof_point: Thinking Machines Lab said Inkling is open-weights, multimodal, available on Tinker today, and also offered through multiple deployment partners and Hugging Face.
search_phrases: multimodal open-weights model Tinker Hugging Face
story_assessments.score_reason: Strong fit for practical AI users because it is newly available, customizable and easy to explain quickly. It is slightly less debate-heavy than the security or compute stories, but very clear and actionable.

story_assessments.story_title: GitHub is moving AI into security review, not just code generation
story_assessments.target_audience: Developers, engineering leads and small software teams
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want faster code review without giving up basic security checks or changing the merge process too much.
story_assessments.interest_signal: GitHub is showing AI security detections directly inside pull requests.
story_assessments.stop_reason: This changes where AI sits in the workflow: inside the review step people already use.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: Teams can test AI-assisted security review as a lightweight assist layer rather than a hard gate.
story_assessments.proof_point: GitHub said the detections run when a pull request is opened or updated, appear in the pull request, and are informational rather than merge-blocking.
search_phrases: GitHub AI security detections pull requests
code scanning AI public preview GitHub
AI security review in pull requests
story_assessments.score_reason: This is highly specific, operationally useful and easy to search. It also has a clear before-and-after workflow story, which makes it strong for both professionals and short-form explanation.

story_assessments.story_title: Reflection’s $1 billion compute deal shows how expensive AI scale still is
story_assessments.target_audience: Founders, AI buyers, operators and practical tech watchers
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want to understand why AI tools, training and availability can change so quickly.
story_assessments.interest_signal: A startup reportedly locked in more than $1 billion of compute capacity, including Nvidia’s latest chips.
story_assessments.stop_reason: The number is large enough to make the hidden cost of AI scale instantly legible.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: It helps readers think about availability, pricing and fallback plans instead of assuming every model will stay cheap and easy to access.
story_assessments.proof_point: Reuters reported that Reflection signed a more than $1 billion computing deal with Nebius and had already signed a June agreement with SpaceX for compute.
search_phrases: Reuters AI startup compute capacity Nvidia latest chips
story_assessments.score_reason: Very strong as a market signal and good for discussion, but it is less immediately hands-on than the product and workflow stories. It fits best when the audience cares about the business side of AI.

story_assessments.story_title: ASML’s higher forecast is another signal that AI demand is still hitting the hardware layer
story_assessments.target_audience: Investors, supply-chain watchers, tech professionals and hardware-minded readers
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want to understand whether AI growth is still constrained by the chipmaking stack.
story_assessments.interest_signal: ASML raised its 2026 forecast as AI demand strengthened, and Intel will use its High-NA tool for some Panther Lake chips.
story_assessments.stop_reason: It shows the AI story reaching deep into the manufacturing layer, not just software launches.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: Readers get a clearer picture of where AI scaling can still bottleneck and what to watch in the hardware chain.
story_assessments.proof_point: Reuters reported that ASML raised its 2026 revenue forecast, cited AI demand for stronger second-quarter earnings, and said Intel will use ASML’s High-NA tool for some Panther Lake chips.
search_phrases: High-NA tool Panther Lake Intel ASML
story_assessments.score_reason: Important macro signal, but the practical takeaway is more indirect for a general audience. It is better for hardware and market watchers than for broad social distribution.

tiktok.story_title: Thinking Machines makes open weights newly visible again
tiktok.target_audience: Creators, small teams, AI builders and learners who want more control over models
tiktok.opening: If you want an AI model you can actually customize, this one is worth a look.
tiktok.payoff: Thinking Machines released Inkling as an open-weights multimodal model, and it is available on Tinker today. That matters because it gives you something you can fine-tune instead of only prompt.
tiktok.selection_reason: TikTok rewards a concrete, visually understandable tool story with a fast payoff. Open weights and customization are easy to grasp in one pass, and the practical question is instantly clear.

youtube.story_title: GitHub is moving AI into security review, not just code generation
youtube.target_audience: Developers, engineering leads and small software teams
youtube.opening: Can AI help catch security issues in pull requests without blocking your merge process?
youtube.payoff: GitHub says its AI-powered security detections now run when a pull request is opened or updated, appear directly in the pull request, and are informational rather than merge-blocking.
youtube.selection_reason: YouTube Shorts works well when the opening is a real workflow question and the answer is specific. This story is searchable, practical and easy to explain in under a minute.

facebook.story_title: Reflection’s $1 billion compute deal shows how expensive AI scale still is
facebook.target_audience: Founders, AI buyers, operators and practical tech watchers
facebook.opening: Have you ever chosen a tool and then worried whether the service behind it could keep up with demand?
facebook.payoff: Reuters reported that Reflection signed a more than $1 billion compute deal with Nebius, which is a reminder that AI tools depend on expensive infrastructure and that availability and pricing can matter as much as model quality.
facebook.selection_reason: Facebook does well with recognizable real-world tradeoffs and a question people can answer from experience. This story naturally leads to a practical discussion about reliability, cost and fallback plans.

pinterest.story_title: Thinking Machines makes open weights newly visible again
pinterest.target_audience: Creators, small teams, AI builders and learners who want more control over models
pinterest.opening: Open-Weights AI Model Checklist: What to Test Before You Fine-Tune
pinterest.payoff: A practical guide for deciding whether a customizable multimodal model is a better fit for your workflow, with a focus on control, fit and a small pilot you can actually run.
pinterest.selection_reason: Pinterest favors searchable, evergreen utility. This story supports a clean guide-style title and a benefit-driven description that people can save for later.

linkedin.story_title: GitHub is moving AI into security review, not just code generation
linkedin.target_audience: Developers, engineering leads and small software teams
linkedin.opening: The next useful AI layer in software is not just code generation; it is review-time control.
linkedin.payoff: GitHub’s AI security detections now appear inside pull requests and are informational rather than merge-blocking, which means teams can test AI-assisted review without changing their release process overnight.
linkedin.selection_reason: LinkedIn is strongest when the lesson is operational. This story has a clear workplace consequence: AI is moving deeper into the review layer, where teams care about speed, control and risk.

overall_reasoning: The strongest audience-fit stories are the ones with a concrete, immediate workflow consequence: the open-weights release and GitHub’s AI security preview. Those two also translate best across short-form and professional platforms because they are easy to explain without inventing any extra context. The compute-deal story is valuable for discussion and market interpretation, while the ASML story is a broader hardware signal that is more indirect for general social audiences. Each platform selection is matched to its native interest graph: TikTok gets a customizable model hook, YouTube Shorts gets a searchable workflow question, Facebook gets a recognizable choice-and-tradeoff discussion, Pinterest gets a saveable guide, and LinkedIn gets an operational lesson about AI moving into review rather than only generation.
